prompt.role: You are a senior DevOps engineer specializing in Rust testing frameworks, CI/CD pipelines, and automated testing infrastructure.

task.title: Comprehensive Testing Setup
task.description: Set up a comprehensive testing framework including unit tests, integration tests, test utilities, CI/CD pipeline, and code coverage reporting for quality assurance.

technical_specifications.spec: Create test utilities module with factory functions for test data generation
Configure separate test environment with .env.test file
Automate test database setup with PostgreSQL container management
Integrate tarpaulin for code coverage reporting with HTML output
Create bash scripts for database setup and test execution
Configure GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with caching and quality checks
Set up test isolation with transactions and rollback
Establish coverage targets: 80% line, 70% branch, 90% function

prompt.implementation_details:
Create comprehensive testing infrastructure:
1. Test Utilities Module (src/test_utils.rs): - Factory function create_test_user(id) for User generation - Test fixtures with proper timestamps - Common test helpers and assertions - Mark with #[cfg(test)] attribute
2. Test Environment Configuration (.env.test): - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://postgres:password@localhost:5432/rust_api_test - RUST_LOG=debug for test diagnostics - Separate from development database
3. Database Setup Script (scripts/setup_test_db.sh): - Check and start PostgreSQL container if needed - Create test database idempotently - Wait for database readiness - Handle container lifecycle
4. Test Execution Script (scripts/run_tests.sh): - Call setup_test_db.sh for environment preparation - Run cargo tarpaulin with HTML output - Generate coverage report in ./coverage directory - Display completion message
5. CI/CD Pipeline (.github/workflows/ci.yml): - Trigger on push and pull_request to main - Set up PostgreSQL service container - Install Rust toolchain with rustfmt and clippy - Cache dependencies using Cargo.lock hash - Install SQLx CLI and run migrations - Execute format check, linting, and tests
6. Coverage Configuration: - Add tarpaulin = "0.25" to dev-dependencies - Generate HTML reports for visualization - Set minimum coverage thresholds

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Test utilities module created with factory functions
Test environment configuration separate from development
Database setup script automates PostgreSQL container
Test execution script combines all test operations
CI/CD pipeline runs on GitHub Actions successfully
All existing tests continue to pass
Scripts are executable with proper permissions
Tests run in isolation with proper cleanup
CI pipeline completes in under 5 minutes

prompt.test_strategy:
Testing Framework Validation:
1. Test Utility Validation: - Verify create_test_user generates valid User objects - Test fixture timestamps are properly set - Utilities are reusable across test suites
2. Script Testing: - Run setup_test_db.sh and verify container starts - Check database is accessible and migrations run - Execute run_tests.sh and verify coverage generation - Test idempotency of database setup
3. CI/CD Validation: - Push to feature branch to trigger workflow - Verify all workflow steps execute successfully - Check dependency caching works - Ensure quality checks (fmt, clippy) run
4. Coverage Validation: - Generate coverage report locally - View HTML report in browser - Verify coverage metrics are accurate - Check exclusions are applied correctly
5. Environment Isolation: - Run tests with test database - Verify no impact on development database - Check test data cleanup after runs - Validate transaction rollback works
Manual validation checklist: - ./scripts/setup_test_db.sh runs successfully - cargo test passes all tests - ./scripts/run_tests.sh generates coverage - Coverage report viewable at ./coverage/tarpaulin-report.html - GitHub Actions workflow succeeds
prompt.instructions:
Think step-by-step about building a comprehensive testing infrastructure. Consider the existing codebase and its testing needs.
Start by creating the test utilities module that will be used across all test suites. Design factory functions that generate consistent, valid test data.
Set up the test environment configuration to ensure complete isolation from development and production databases. Use environment variables for all configuration.
Create robust bash scripts that handle all edge cases - container already running, database already exists, network issues, etc. Make scripts idempotent so they can be run repeatedly.
Design the CI/CD pipeline to be fast and reliable. Use caching effectively, run checks in parallel where possible, and provide clear failure messages.
Configure code coverage to provide actionable insights. Set realistic thresholds that encourage good testing practices without being overly restrictive.
Write clear documentation for all scripts and configurations. Other developers should be able to understand and run the tests without assistance.
Test the entire setup thoroughly - run scripts multiple times, trigger CI pipeline with various scenarios, verify coverage reports are accurate.
Ensure the testing framework scales with the project - it should handle more tests, more complex scenarios, and parallel execution as the codebase grows.
